chunks.text: The performance of NumPy arrays and Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: The second thing we should know about NumPy arrays

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: That is NumPy arrays store data in continuous memory blocks

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Because we have homogeneous data in NumPy arrays

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So we can store the data in continuous memory blocks

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And this storage is not in continuous memory blocks in Python

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Means we can store our data in this format

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Like if we have stored data of 1,2,3,4 numbers

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And let's suppose in memory this is address 100

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So this will be address 104

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: This will be address 108

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: This will be address 112

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: The difference we are taking is the difference of bytes

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: We are storing one integer in 4 bytes

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So this is how our data is stored

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: But the Python list is not stored in this way

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: It is stored on different memory addresses

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: There are no continuous memory addresses like this

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Plus NumPy arrays use less memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: That is why they have optimized storage

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So if we want we can compare their performance in terms of memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Ok so to compare the memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: What are we going to do

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: We are going to print the size of both Python list and NumPy array in terms of bytes

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So we will know how much memory Python list occupies

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And how much memory NumPy arrays occupy

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So first of all we will print the size of Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So for that we will have to actually import a package which is called sys

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So we are going to print the size of Python list first

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Python list size is equal to

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: This is actually equal to system.getSizeOf the Python list that we have

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And we will multiply it with the length

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So length of Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And we will get it in terms of bytes

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So we will execute it and it will come to us

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And in the same way if we want

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: In fact let's copy this

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: We can also print the size of our NumPy array

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So this is for NumPy array

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And NumPy arrays actually have a direct property

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Which is npErr.nbytes

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So if we print this we can see the difference

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And it is actually a lot of difference

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Means there is a difference of multiples

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So we are getting to know that NumPy arrays use the memory very efficiently

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: In an optimized way

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Whereas Python list occupies a lot of memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And why is this extra occupying of memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Because in Python list a lot of additional metadata is stored

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Apart from actual data

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: One is that all the data is not stored at once

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So which data is stored in the Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Extra information has to be stored in the memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Plus with each data

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Extra information of this type

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Extra metadata has to be stored

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: That is why the storage is more in Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Plus in NumPy we have built-in mathematical functions

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Which help us a lot for numerical computations

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: And NumPy also supports direct vectorized operations

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: We have already discussed vectorized operations

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Many operations which are in the list by loop

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: They are directly in NumPy arrays

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So that is why NumPy arrays are much better

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Especially when we talk about numerical data as compared to Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: So why is NumPy faster

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Number one it has homogeneous data types and contiguous memory

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: We will read about the data types of NumPy in detail

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Plus it has vector operations and C implementations

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: Plus our NumPy has reduced overhead

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: It does not need to store extra metadata like Python list

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: That is why it is faster

chunks.title: Python
chunks.text: We do not have to process that much data
